summary.intent: Fix a series of Codex review findings on PR #710 (impl-285 branch) in the fogodev/ars-ui repo, primarily around GridList controlled selection state, grid dimension calculations, and keyboard event handling.
summary.outcome: All three original Codex threads were resolved and replied to with commit 4fc496d7. A follow-up Codex finding about controlled selection rendering was also fixed (splitting interaction vs. render selection sources), committed, and pushed. CI passed formatting, Clippy, and coverage (ars-components branch coverage at 90.1%).

repo: GridList must compute dimensions, cell positions, range selection, and 2D navigation over real item nodes only — sections and separators must not consume grid cells.
Missing or non-item keys must be treated as disabled in cell_attrs, keeping stale non-composite cells at tabindex=-1 with disabled state.
The convenience keydown helper should use the std platform clock when available, falling back to last-key-plus-1ms only when no clock is available.

Bindable has two distinct uses: requested_selected_keys carries the pending outbound interaction request for parent notification, while selected_keys.get() is the authoritative render source when a prop is controlled. These must not be conflated.
ars-components branch coverage threshold is 90%, and the project uses cargo xcov for coverage reporting.
The project uses cargo xtask ci clippy for Clippy checks and cargo +nightly fmt for formatting.

code.finding: selected_keys_source was used for both interaction calculations and rendering; it was split into interaction_selected_keys (pending request) and rendered_selected_keys (controlled bound value) to correctly separate concerns.
cell_attrs helper needed to handle missing/non-item keys as disabled to avoid stale non-composite cells retaining active tabindex.

Regression test added asserting both sides of controlled selection: pending request remains available on requested_selected_keys, while row/cell attrs render only the controlled parent selection via Bindable::get().
Bindable struct/enum/impl is defined here — get() returns the controlled (parent-bound) value.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via gh api, and progress is tracked via the 'eyes' reaction on the issue.
Codex review threads can be resolved programmatically via the GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation using thread IDs (PRRT_... format).
Replies to specific PR review comments are posted via gh api -X POST repos/.../pulls/{pr}/comments/{comment_id}/replies.
Polling CI status with 'gh pr checks {pr}' and review threads with a GraphQL query filtering for unresolved codex-authored threads is an effective babysitting pattern.
rustfmt wrapping issues in new assertion blocks are common and caught by cargo +nightly fmt --check before CI.

friction: Multiple CI polling cycles (several sleep 60/90/120 intervals) were needed to wait for long-tail jobs like coverage, unit tests, release verification, and feature-flag matrix groups.
Codex surfaced an additional controlled-selection rendering bug after the initial three fixes were already pushed, requiring another fix-commit-push cycle.
The commit hook added latency before the push could proceed.
open_items: Verify that the final commit (controlled-selection fix) passes all CI checks and receives a clean Codex review with no new open threads.
PR #710 is not yet merged — it is awaiting full CI green and final Codex sign-off.
